title: Sphere Packing Bounds Via Spherical Codes
speaker: Henry Cohn
abstract: We develop a simple geometric variant of the Kabatiansky-Levenshtein approach to proving sphere packing density bounds. This variant gives a small improvement to the best bounds known in Euclidean space (from 1978) and an exponential improvement in hyperbolic space. Furthermore, we show how to achieve the same results via the Cohn-Elkies linear programming bounds, and we formulate a few problems in harmonic analysis that could lead to even better bounds. This is joint work with Yufei Zhao.

proof_stages.label: Euclidean packing density and asymptotic gap
proof_stages.summary: The talk defines Euclidean sphere-packing density, reviews the known low-dimensional cases and the huge high-dimensional gap between the Minkowski-type lower bound near 2^{-n} and the Kabatiansky-Levenshtein upper bound near 2^{-0.599n}, and sets the goal of understanding upper bounds.

proof_stages.label: Saturated-packing lower-bound benchmark
proof_stages.summary: As background, a saturated packing argument shows density at least 2^{-n} by doubling all ball radii to cover space, with later refinements improving only subexponential or polynomial factors.

proof_stages.label: Hyperbolic-density side formulation
proof_stages.summary: The hyperbolic analogue is explained as delicate because boundary growth destroys naive density limits, while the Bowen-Radin invariant-measure formulation gives a robust density notion to which local and KL-type upper-bound methods can be applied.

proof_stages.label: Reduction from Euclidean packings to spherical codes
proof_stages.summary: Spherical codes A(n,theta) are introduced, the Kabatiansky-Levenshtein reduction Delta_n <= sin(theta/2)^n A(n+1,theta) is recalled, and the joint improvement to A(n,theta) is proved by averaging a radius-R ball over a Euclidean packing and radially projecting the captured centers.

proof_stages.label: Delsarte linear programming bound on the sphere
proof_stages.summary: Positive definite kernels on the sphere, their Schoenberg-Gegenbauer characterization, and the average value g-bar are set up in order to state the Delsarte-Goethals-Seidel theorem bounding spherical code size by g(1)/g-bar under a sign condition.

proof_stages.label: Signed-measure proof of the spherical LP bound
proof_stages.summary: For a spherical code C, the signed measure consisting of point masses at codewords plus lambda times surface measure is inserted into the positive-definite quadratic form, the off-diagonal terms are discarded using the sign condition, and optimizing over lambda yields |C| <= g(1)/g-bar.

proof_stages.label: Direct Euclidean linear programming bound
proof_stages.summary: The Euclidean Cohn-Elkies linear programming theorem is presented: a positive-definite function f on R^n that is nonpositive beyond distance 2 bounds packing density by vol(B_1^n) f(0)/f-hat(0), paralleling the spherical LP theorem but acting directly in Euclidean space.

proof_stages.label: Transformation from spherical LP to Euclidean LP and prospects
proof_stages.summary: The joint work with Yufei Zhao constructs an integral transformation sending every admissible spherical auxiliary function g to a Euclidean auxiliary function f with the same resulting density bound, recovering KL and the slight improvement while suggesting that truly optimal Euclidean functions should not arise by going through spheres.
